ResourceType.description: Non-valuable materials collected during the mining process.
A chemically reactive, gray metal that is malleable, lightweight, ductile, strong, and resistant to corrosion. Capable of superconductivity.
This highly thermally conductive material is often formed from cosmic ray spallation and is most typically found in younger star systems.
A commonly found ductile metal favored for its conductivity. Though it falls in and out of fashion, it is often used for decorative purposes as well.
Extremely conductive, this mineral was first discovered when a researcher noticed a breed of electrosensitive hermit crab using pieces of it to form its shell.
This mineral harvested from worlds with no natural magnetic core, has found widespread use in electrical systems.
A highly unstable mineral used in the production of quantum fuel.
